content.text: Self-Hosted Observability Platform

A **production-grade observability stack** built from scratch on a self-hosted k3s Kubernetes cluster — monitoring every layer of the infrastructure in real time. Prometheus scrapes hardware and container metrics every 10 seconds, Grafana visualizes them internally, and a custom Next.js dashboard exposes a public-facing view without authentication.

This was the first DevOps project on the homelab — setting up full observability before deploying any other workload. The principle: **you can't manage what you can't measure**. Before running n8n, LLaMA, or any other service, the monitoring stack went in first.

content.title: Live Dashboards
content.text: **Public dashboard** - stats.vpjoshi.in (no login) · **Grafana** - grafana.vpjoshi.in (auth protected) · Both running on the homelab k3s cluster, exposed via Cloudflare Tunnel.
The Problem

Running a self-hosted server without observability is flying blind. You don't know if RAM is saturating, if a pod is consuming unexpected CPU, if disk is filling up, or if the server is throttling under load. By the time something breaks, it's too late to understand why.

Grafana Cloud and Datadog solve this but cost money and send your metrics to external servers — defeating the purpose of self-hosting. The goal was a **fully on-premise observability stack** with zero data leaving the homelab and zero ongoing cost.

content.title: Grafana's Limitation
content.text: Grafana dashboards require authentication — there's no native way to share a live public dashboard without giving someone a login. For a homelab, this means server stats can't be shared publicly. A custom public dashboard was needed.
Solution Overview

A two-layer observability stack — **Grafana + Prometheus** for deep internal monitoring, and a **custom Next.js dashboard** for public-facing metrics. Both run as K8s deployments on the same cluster they're monitoring, exposed via Cloudflare Tunnel.

items: **Prometheus** - Scrapes `node_exporter` (hardware) and `scaphandre` (power) every 10 seconds. Stores time-series metrics locally.
**Grafana** - Full internal dashboard at grafana.vpjoshi.in. Uses the Node Exporter Full dashboard (ID: 1860) — 200+ panels covering every hardware and OS metric.
**stats.vpjoshi.in** - Custom Next.js 15 app that queries Prometheus server-side and renders a public dashboard. No auth wall, no Grafana dependency.
**node_exporter** - Exposes CPU, RAM, disk, network, pressure, and context switch metrics from the host.
**scaphandre** - Exposes real-time power draw in microwatts. Used to calculate live electricity cost.
**Cloudflare Tunnel** - Both dashboards exposed publicly with no open ports on the home router.

content.title: Monitoring the Monitor
content.text: Prometheus itself is monitored — it scrapes its own `/metrics` endpoint. If Prometheus goes down, Grafana alerts. The stack is self-aware.
System Architecture

content.title: No Open Ports
content.text: Neither Grafana nor stats.vpjoshi.in have open ports on the home router. **Cloudflare Tunnel** creates a secure outbound-only connection — all traffic is proxied through Cloudflare with automatic TLS.
Kubernetes Deployment

Every component of the observability stack runs as a K8s workload on k3s. Prometheus and Grafana run in the `monitoring` namespace. `node_exporter` runs as a **DaemonSet** — one pod per node, guaranteed — so it always has access to host-level metrics regardless of scheduling.

content.code:
# node_exporter — DaemonSet (one pod per node, host network)
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: node-exporter
  namespace: monitoring
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: node-exporter
  template:
    spec:
      hostNetwork: true       # access host network metrics
      hostPID: true           # access host process metrics
      containers:
        - name: node-exporter
          image: prom/node-exporter:latest
          args:
            - '--path.rootfs=/host'
          volumeMounts:
            - name: host-root
              mountPath: /host
              readOnly: true
      volumes:
        - name: host-root
          hostPath:
            path: /

# Prometheus — Deployment with PVC for metric storage
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: prometheus
  namespace: monitoring
spec:
  replicas: 1
  template:
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: prometheus
          image: prom/prometheus:latest
          args:
            - '--config.file=/etc/prometheus/prometheus.yml'
            - '--storage.tsdb.path=/prometheus'
            - '--storage.tsdb.retention.time=30d'
          volumeMounts:
            - name: config
              mountPath: /etc/prometheus
            - name: storage
              mountPath: /prometheus
      volumes:
        - name: storage
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: prometheus-pvc    # local-path PVC on k3s

content.title: 30-Day Retention
content.text: Prometheus is configured with `--storage.tsdb.retention.time=30d` — 30 days of metric history stored on the local SSD. Enough for trend analysis and incident retrospectives without consuming too much of the 128GB disk.
Prometheus Configuration

Prometheus is configured to scrape three targets — `node_exporter` for hardware metrics, `scaphandre` for power draw, and itself for meta-monitoring. All scrape intervals are 10 seconds for high-resolution time series.

content.code:
# prometheus.yml
global:
  scrape_interval: 10s
  evaluation_interval: 10s

scrape_configs:
  - job_name: 'node-exporter'
    static_configs:
      - targets: ['localhost:9100']
    relabel_configs:
      - source_labels: [__address__]
        target_label: instance
        replacement: 'homelab'

  - job_name: 'scaphandre'
    static_configs:
      - targets: ['localhost:8080']

  - job_name: 'prometheus'
    static_configs:
      - targets: ['localhost:9090']

content.text: Key PromQL Queries
These are the core queries powering both Grafana panels and the stats.vpjoshi.in API routes:

content.code:
# CPU Load %
100 - (avg(rate(node_cpu_seconds_total{mode="idle"}[1m])) * 100)

# CPU Temperature
node_hwmon_temp_celsius{chip=~".*coretemp.*", sensor="temp1"}

# Live Power Draw (watts)
scaph_host_power_microwatts / 1000000

# Avg Power last 1hr
avg_over_time(scaph_host_power_microwatts[1h:10s]) / 1000000

# RAM Usage %
(1 - (node_memory_MemAvailable_bytes / node_memory_MemTotal_bytes)) * 100

# Disk Used %
(1 - (node_filesystem_avail_bytes{mountpoint="/"} /
  node_filesystem_size_bytes{mountpoint="/"})) * 100

# Network I/O (KB/s)
rate(node_network_receive_bytes_total{device="eth0"}[1m]) / 1024
rate(node_network_transmit_bytes_total{device="eth0"}[1m]) / 1024

# Session uptime
time() - node_boot_time_seconds

# CPU Pressure (PSI)
rate(node_pressure_cpu_waiting_seconds_total[1m])

# Context switches/s
rate(node_context_switches_total[1m])

content.text: Grafana - Internal Monitoring Dashboard

content.caption: Node Exporter Full dashboard (ID: 1860) — 200+ panels covering every hardware and OS metric.

content.text: **Grafana** runs at `grafana.vpjoshi.in` (auth protected) as a K8s deployment in the `monitoring` namespace. It connects to the Prometheus datasource on the same cluster via the internal K8s service DNS — no external network hop.
Node Exporter Full Dashboard

The primary dashboard is the community **Node Exporter Full** (Grafana dashboard ID: 1860) — imported directly from Grafana's dashboard registry. It provides 200+ pre-built panels covering every metric exposed by node_exporter.

items: **CPU** - per-core usage, load average, steal time, iowait, system/user split.
**Memory** - RAM usage, swap, page faults, memory pressure (PSI).
**Disk** - read/write throughput, IOPS, disk utilization %, filesystem usage.
**Network** - in/out throughput per interface, errors, drops, TCP connections.
**System** - context switches/s, interrupts/s, processes, file descriptors.
**Hardware** - CPU temperature per core (via hwmon), power draw (scaphandre).
**Pressure** - Linux PSI (Pressure Stall Information) for CPU, memory, and IO.

content.code:
# Grafana datasource — connects to Prometheus via K8s internal DNS
apiVersion: 1
datasources:
  - name: Prometheus
    type: prometheus
    url: http://prometheus-svc.monitoring.svc.cluster.local:9090
    isDefault: true
    jsonData:
      timeInterval: '10s'

content.title: Internal DNS
content.text: Grafana reaches Prometheus via K8s internal service DNS (`prometheus-svc.monitoring.svc.cluster.local`) — no external network hop, no Cloudflare Tunnel involved. Internal cluster traffic stays inside the cluster.
stats.vpjoshi.in - Public Monitoring Dashboard

content.caption: Public dashboard — live CPU, RAM, power draw, uptime, electricity cost, and network I/O. No login required.

content.text: A custom **Next.js 15** app that queries Prometheus server-side via API routes and renders a public-facing metrics page. Built because Grafana dashboards require authentication — this exposes live server stats to anyone at `stats.vpjoshi.in` with no auth wall.
Server-Side Query Architecture

`PROMETHEUS_URL` is an environment variable only available server-side. The browser never talks to Prometheus directly — it only hits `/api/metrics/*` routes on the same origin. This keeps the internal Prometheus port private even though the dashboard is public.
Metrics on Display

items: **CPU load %** - averaged over last 1 minute via PromQL rate.
**CPU temperature** - from `node_hwmon_temp_celsius` (hwmon coretemp driver).
**Live power draw** - `scaph_host_power_microwatts` converted to watts.
**Avg power (1hr)** - `avg_over_time` subquery over the last hour.
**Est. monthly electricity cost** - avg watts × 24h × 30d ÷ 1000 × ₹6/unit.
**RAM usage %** - MemAvailable vs MemTotal.
**Disk used %** - filesystem avail vs total at mountpoint `/`.
**Swap usage** - SwapFree vs SwapTotal.
**Session uptime** - `time() - node_boot_time_seconds`.
**Total uptime** - hardcoded start date (1 Feb 2026) vs `Date.now()`.
**Network I/O** - in/out KB/s via `rate(node_network_*_bytes_total[1m])`.
**Load avg 1m/5m** - `node_load1`, `node_load5`.
**CPU/RAM pressure (PSI)** - Linux pressure stall information.
**Context switches/s** - `rate(node_context_switches_total[1m])`.
**Disk read/write KB/s** - `rate(node_disk_*_bytes_total[1m])`.
**Network drops** - `rate(node_network_receive_drop_total[1m])`.

content.text: SWR Refresh & CI/CD

The frontend uses **SWR** for data fetching with a configurable refresh interval selectable from the UI (10s → 5m). The refresh interval is passed to both `useStats()` and each chart component so everything refreshes in sync. Deployed to k3s via GitHub Actions on `[prod]` commit messages — same CI/CD pattern as all other homelab services.

content.title: Single Endpoint Design
content.text: All 20+ stat card values are fetched in a **single `Promise.all`** via `/api/metrics/stats` — one round trip instead of 20 individual requests. Charts have their own endpoints (`/api/metrics/temp`, `/api/metrics/power`, etc.) since they poll on different schedules.
Cloudflare Tunnel Ingress

Both Grafana and stats.vpjoshi.in are exposed via **Cloudflare Tunnel** — a secure outbound-only connection from the homelab to Cloudflare's edge. No ports are open on the home router. No public IP is needed. TLS is handled automatically by Cloudflare.

content.code:
# cloudflared tunnel config
ingress:
  - hostname: grafana.vpjoshi.in
    service: http://grafana-svc.monitoring.svc.cluster.local:3000

  - hostname: stats.vpjoshi.in
    service: http://stats-svc.monitoring.svc.cluster.local:3000

  - service: http_status:404

content.text: The tunnel routes traffic from Cloudflare's edge directly to the K8s internal service DNS — bypassing Traefik for monitoring services. This isolates the monitoring stack from the main ingress controller, so a Traefik misconfiguration can't accidentally expose or break Grafana.

content.title: Grafana Auth
content.text: Even though Grafana is behind Cloudflare Tunnel, it still requires **Grafana's own login**. Two layers of protection — Cloudflare blocks DDoS and bot traffic at the edge, Grafana enforces user authentication at the application level.
Full Technical Stack
Metrics Collection

items: **Prometheus** - Time-series metrics storage and PromQL query engine. 30-day retention.
**node_exporter** - Exposes 200+ hardware and OS metrics from the host (CPU, RAM, disk, network, pressure, hwmon).
**scaphandre** - Real-time power draw in microwatts via RAPL (Running Average Power Limit).

content.text: Internal Dashboard

items: **Grafana** - Full monitoring dashboard at grafana.vpjoshi.in (auth protected).
**Node Exporter Full (ID: 1860)** - 200+ panel community dashboard, imported from Grafana registry.

content.text: Public Dashboard

items: **Next.js 15 (App Router)** - Server-side Prometheus queries via API routes.
**TypeScript 5** - Full type safety across components, hooks, and API responses.
**Tailwind CSS v4** - Dark theme UI.
**Recharts** - AreaChart for all time-series graphs (temp, power, RAM, network).
**SWR** - Configurable refresh interval (10s → 5m), UI-controlled.

content.text: Infrastructure

items: **k3s** - Lightweight Kubernetes, all components run as K8s workloads.
**DaemonSet** - node_exporter runs as a DaemonSet with `hostNetwork: true` for host-level access.
**Cloudflare Tunnel** - Secure public exposure for both Grafana and stats dashboard.
**GitHub Actions** - CI/CD for stats.vpjoshi.in, deploys on `[prod]` commit messages.
**Persistent Volume** - Prometheus metric storage on local-path PVC, 30-day retention.

content.text: Key DevOps Lessons
Observability First

The monitoring stack was deployed before any other service on the homelab. This meant every new workload — n8n, LLaMA, PocketBase, Postgres — was observable from day one. RAM pressure during LLaMA inference, disk I/O spikes during n8n workflows, and network drops were all visible immediately.
DaemonSet vs Deployment

node_exporter must run as a **DaemonSet** with `hostNetwork: true` and `hostPID: true` — not a regular Deployment. A Deployment could be scheduled on any node and might miss host-level metrics. A DaemonSet guarantees exactly one pod per node with full host access.
Internal DNS for Datasources

Grafana connects to Prometheus via **K8s internal service DNS** (`prometheus-svc.monitoring.svc.cluster.local`) — not via `localhost` or an external URL. This works regardless of which node each pod is scheduled on, and keeps all metric traffic inside the cluster.
Server-Side Prometheus Queries

The public stats dashboard queries Prometheus **server-side only** — `PROMETHEUS_URL` is never exposed to the browser. Even though the dashboard is fully public, the internal Prometheus port and network address are never revealed to clients.

content.title: MTU & DNS Gotchas
content.text: Two real issues hit during setup: an **MTU mismatch** (fixed via `FLANNEL_MTU=1450`) caused silent packet drops on cross-pod traffic. **ISP-injected search domains** leaked into CoreDNS via `/etc/resolv.conf`, breaking internal DNS resolution — fixed by symlinking to the non-stub resolver and overriding Netplan DHCP DNS settings.
